reasoning: The die(__FILE__ . ':' . __LINE__) at class-connection.php line 99 is confirmed to exist in the code and will output the full server filesystem path on a nonce failure. However, the path to this sink requires the caller to be an authenticated WordPress administrator (manage_options capability). The entry point is Connection::display(), registered as an admin menu page callback via add_menu_page() with $capability = 'manage_options' in class-omnisend-core-bootstrap.php line 126. WordPress core enforces that capability before invoking the callback, and display() adds a redundant explicit check (current_user_can('manage_options')) before any logic runs. show_connection_view() is only called from display() after those gates. The die() fires only when $_GET['action'] === 'show_connection_form' AND the nonce is invalid. The finding's claim that 'any visitor' can reach this is wrong — only admins can. An admin who sees the path leak already has filesystem-level visibility via other means (file manager, wp-cli, etc.), so the practical impact is negligible. The sink is real but severity should be downgraded from low to informational/negligible due to the admin-only reachability constraint.
